enrichment.short_description: Open-source, cookieless web analytics platform with session replay, funnels, and real-time dashboards.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Rybbit is an open-source web analytics platform positioned as a modern, privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. It tracks visitors without cookies and offers a real-time dashboard, session replay, conversion funnels, user journey mapping, and Core Web Vitals monitoring.

The code is fully open source and available on GitHub under an AGPL-3.0 license, so teams can self-host it on their own infrastructure. Rybbit also offers a managed cloud version hosted on EU infrastructure in Germany for teams that don't want to run their own servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Rybbit suits website owners and product teams who want detailed, privacy-compliant analytics (GDPR/CCPA) without relying on Google Analytics, and who value the option to self-host their analytics stack or use a paid cloud plan for convenience and session replay.

features.title: Real-time dashboard
features.description: Live visitor tracking and instant updates to traffic data as it happens.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Watch recorded user sessions to see how visitors actually interact with a site (available on paid plans).

features.title: Conversion funnels & user journeys
features.description: Visualize where visitors drop off and map full navigation paths through a site.

features.title: Web Vitals monitoring
features.description: Tracks Core Web Vitals performance metrics for site optimization.

features.title: Cookieless, GDPR/CCPA-compliant tracking
features.description: Collects analytics data without cookies or personal-data-based tracking.

features.title: Custom events & bot blocking
features.description: Track custom conversion events like sign-ups and purchases, with filtering to keep bot traffic out of reports.

features.title: Full API access
features.description: REST API for building custom integrations and pulling analytics data programmatically.

pros: Open source and self-hostable at no cost
No cookies required, built for GDPR/CCPA compliance
Real-time dashboard with funnels, journeys, and Web Vitals in one tool
Affordable entry-level paid tier compared to many analytics competitors
EU-hosted cloud option for data residency
cons: Session replay is limited to the Pro tier and above
Cloud pricing scales with number of sites and team members
On-premise installation and SSO are gated behind custom Enterprise pricing
Self-hosting requires running and maintaining your own Docker/ClickHouse infrastructure

faqs.question: Is Rybbit free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted version is free and open source under AGPL-3.0. Rybbit also offers a paid cloud version starting at $13/month for teams that don't want to self-host.

faqs.question: Is Rybbit open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, the full codebase is available on GitHub (rybbit-io/rybbit) under an AGPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Does Rybbit use cookies?
faqs.answer: No, Rybbit is designed as a cookieless analytics tool aimed at GDPR and CCPA compliance.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Rybbit?
faqs.answer: Yes, Rybbit ships with Docker Compose configurations for self-hosted deployment, or you can use their managed cloud offering.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious website tracking
use_cases.description: Track site traffic and behavior without cookies or invasive user tracking.

use_cases.title: Product analytics with session replay
use_cases.description: Use funnels, journeys, and session replay to understand user behavior in a web app.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted enterprise analytics
use_cases.description: Run Rybbit on-premise with SSO and infinite retention for compliance-sensitive organizations.

features: Custom events, funnels, goals, journeys, Web Vitals

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Rybbit is an open-source, cookieless web analytics platform with real-time dashboards, session replay, and funnels.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, SQL-first BI tool powered by DuckDB for building dashboards, PDF reports, and embedded analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Shaper is an open-source data platform for building dashboards, PDF reports, and data exports entirely by writing SQL, powered by DuckDB as its query engine. It runs as a single Docker container and supports querying across multiple data sources, including Postgres, S3, and Google Sheets, via DuckDB extensions.

Built by Taleshape OÜ, Shaper was made open source under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 in August 2025. Beyond the free self-hosted core, Taleshape offers managed hosting and support plans (Standard, Pro, and Enterprise) for teams that want a fully-managed instance instead of running the infrastructure themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Shaper is aimed at developers and data teams comfortable writing SQL who want a 'BI-as-code' workflow — dashboards defined in version-controlled SQL files, deployable through CI/CD — plus the ability to embed dashboards into their own products via JS/React SDKs with JWT-based access control.

features.title: SQL-first dashboards
features.description: Build interactive charts and dashboards by writing SQL with special syntax that controls visualization type.

features.title: DuckDB-powered multi-source querying
features.description: Query Postgres, S3, Google Sheets, and other sources through DuckDB extensions.

features.title: Embedded analytics SDKs
features.description: JS and React SDKs with JWT-based row-level security for embedding dashboards into your own product.

features.title: Automated reporting & exports
features.description: Generate PDF, PNG, CSV, and Excel exports, plus scheduled alerts and password-protected shareable links.

features.title: Git-based workflow
features.description: Manage dashboards as SQL files under version control, deployable via CI/CD pipelines.

features.title: Self-hosted single-container deployment
features.description: Runs as one Docker container (`docker run ... taleshape/shaper`) for quick self-hosted setup.

pros: Open source and self-hostable in a single Docker container
No vendor lock-in — you can take your server and data with you
Powerful, flexible SQL-first approach built on DuckDB
Built-in embedding and white-labeling for customer-facing analytics
Git-based, version-controlled 'BI-as-code' workflow
cons: Requires SQL knowledge; not a no-code/drag-and-drop tool
Newer project (open-sourced in 2025) with a smaller community than established BI tools
Managed hosting plan pricing is not publicly listed and requires booking a demo

faqs.question: Is Shaper free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core product is open source under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 and free to self-host. Taleshape also offers paid managed hosting and support plans.

faqs.question: What database does Shaper use?
faqs.answer: Shaper is powered by DuckDB and can query multiple external data sources through DuckDB extensions.

faqs.question: Can Shaper dashboards be embedded in other apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Shaper provides JS and React SDKs with JWT-based access control for embedding dashboards into your own product.

faqs.question: Who built Shaper?
faqs.answer: Shaper is built by Taleshape OÜ, founded by Jorin Vogel.

use_cases.title: Customer-facing embedded analytics
use_cases.description: Embed white-labeled dashboards directly into a SaaS product using the JS/React SDKs.

use_cases.title: BI-as-code workflows
use_cases.description: Define and version-control dashboards as SQL files, deployed through CI/CD.
use_cases.audience: Data engineers and technical data teams

use_cases.title: Automated reporting
use_cases.description: Generate scheduled PDF, CSV, and Excel reports and shareable links from SQL queries.

features: AI data analyst / MCP server integration

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Shaper is an open-source, SQL-first BI tool built on DuckDB for dashboards, PDF reports, and embedded analytics.

enrichment.short_description: Free, Apache-licensed open-source platform for data exploration, SQL querying, and building BI dashboards.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Apache Superset is a modern, open-source data exploration and visualization platform. It provides a drag-and-drop chart builder with 40+ visualization types, a SQL Lab editor for advanced querying, a semantic layer for reusable metrics and dimensions, and connectivity to 40+ SQL databases and data engines including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks.

The project began as an internal hackathon project at Airbnb in 2015 and was open sourced the following year. It joined the Apache Incubator in 2017 and became a top-level Apache Software Foundation project in January 2021, coinciding with the 1.0 release. It is licensed under Apache-2.0 and developed by an open community rather than a single company.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Superset is built for data teams and engineers who want a free, self-hosted, highly extensible BI platform with broad database support and a REST API, as an alternative to commercial tools like Tableau or Looker.

features.title: No-code chart builder
features.description: Drag-and-drop interface for rapidly building charts without writing code.

features.title: SQL Lab
features.description: Web-based SQL editor for advanced, ad-hoc querying against connected databases.

features.title: 40+ visualization types
features.description: Pre-installed chart types ranging from basic charts to geospatial visualizations.

features.title: Broad database connectivity
features.description: Connects to 40+ SQL databases and data engines including Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, and PostgreSQL.

features.title: Semantic layer
features.description: Define reusable custom dimensions and metrics on top of raw data.

features.title: Dashboard filtering & cross-filters
features.description: Interactive filtering, drill-down, and cross-filtering across dashboard charts.

features.title: Extensible security & RBAC
features.description: Configurable authentication and role-based access control.

features.title: REST API
features.description: Programmatic access for automation and custom integrations.

pros: Completely free and open source under Apache-2.0
Backed by the Apache Software Foundation with an active community
Supports 40+ database connectors out of the box
Highly extensible and customizable (no vendor lock-in)
No-code chart builder alongside a full SQL editor for advanced users
cons: Requires self-hosting and infrastructure maintenance — there is no official hosted SaaS from the Apache project itself
Setup and configuration require technical/DevOps expertise
Steeper learning curve for non-technical business users compared to some commercial BI tools

faqs.question: Is Apache Superset free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Superset is fully free and open source under the Apache-2.0 license.

faqs.question: Who maintains Apache Superset?
faqs.answer: It is a top-level project of the Apache Software Foundation, developed by an open community; it originated as a hackathon project at Airbnb in 2015.

faqs.question: Does Apache Superset offer a hosted version?
faqs.answer: The Apache project itself does not offer official hosting; third-party companies offer managed Superset hosting separately.

faqs.question: What databases does Superset support?
faqs.answer: It connects to 40+ SQL databases and data engines, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks.

use_cases.title: Internal BI dashboards
use_cases.description: Build and share dashboards across teams using data already stored in existing databases.

use_cases.title: SQL-based data exploration
use_cases.description: Use SQL Lab to run ad-hoc queries against connected databases without a separate SQL client.

use_cases.title: Free alternative to commercial BI tools
use_cases.description: Replace paid tools like Tableau or Looker with a self-hosted, open-source platform.
use_cases.audience: Organizations wanting to avoid BI licensing costs

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apache Superset is a free, open-source BI platform for data exploration, SQL querying, and dashboard visualization.

enrichment.short_description: Privacy-first, open-source web analytics with cookieless tracking, funnels, error tracking, and A/B testing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Swetrix is a privacy-first, cookieless web analytics platform positioned as an alternative to Google Analytics. It tracks pageviews, visitors, referrers, devices, and countries without collecting personal data, and adds custom events and goals, funnels, error tracking, performance monitoring, A/B testing, and feature flags.

The platform is fully open source, with a self-hostable Community Edition (backend built on Nest.js, MySQL, ClickHouse, and Redis) available under the AGPLv3 license, alongside a paid Cloud version hosted on EU infrastructure (Germany, via Hetzner). Swetrix Ltd is a UK-based, bootstrapped, subscriber-funded company operating since 2021.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Swetrix suits privacy-conscious teams who want GDPR-compliant, cookieless analytics along with product experimentation features like A/B testing and feature flags, whether they prefer a managed cloud plan or self-hosting the Community Edition.

features.title: Cookieless, privacy-first tracking
features.description: Tracks traffic without cookies or personal data collection, designed for GDPR compliance.

features.title: Custom events & goals
features.description: Track conversions like sign-ups and purchases with custom event and goal definitions.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Watch recorded user sessions on the Cloud plans to see actual visitor behavior.

features.title: Error tracking
features.description: Automatic detection and aggregated reporting of JavaScript errors.

features.title: Funnels & performance monitoring
features.description: Visualize conversion funnels and monitor real-user page load performance.

features.title: A/B testing & feature flags
features.description: Run experiments and gradual feature rollouts (available on the Plus plan and above).

features.title: Google Search Console integration
features.description: Connects with Search Console for combined SEO and traffic insights.

pros: Open-source Community Edition is free to self-host
Cookieless tracking designed for GDPR compliance
Bootstrapped, subscriber-funded company with a transparent business model
Combines analytics with A/B testing, feature flags, and error tracking in one tool
14-day free trial on paid Cloud plans
cons: Session replay, A/B testing, and feature flags are gated behind paid Cloud plans
Self-hosting requires managing a MySQL + ClickHouse + Redis stack
Cloud pricing scales with monthly event volume
Smaller community compared to more established analytics tools

faqs.question: Is Swetrix free?
faqs.answer: The self-hosted Community Edition is free and open source (AGPLv3). The Cloud version starts at €17/month with a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Is Swetrix GDPR-compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Swetrix uses cookieless, privacy-first tracking designed for GDPR and CCPA compliance.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Swetrix?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition can be self-hosted via Docker with your own MySQL, ClickHouse, and Redis instances.

faqs.question: Where is Swetrix based?
faqs.answer: Swetrix Ltd is based in the UK, with its Cloud infrastructure hosted in Germany via Hetzner.

use_cases.title: GDPR-compliant analytics
use_cases.description: Replace Google Analytics with cookieless, privacy-first tracking.

use_cases.title: Product experimentation
use_cases.description: Run A/B tests and feature flag rollouts alongside standard analytics.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted EU analytics
use_cases.description: Run the open-source Community Edition on your own infrastructure for full data control.

features: Session replays, A/B testing, feature flags

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Swetrix is a privacy-first, open-source web analytics platform with cookieless tracking, funnels, and A/B testing.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted web analytics platform shipped as a single binary — a privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Vince is a self-hosted, open-source web analytics platform built as a privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics. It ships as a single binary with no external runtime dependencies, and can be deployed via Docker, Helm, or a direct binary download.

The project was created by developer Geofrey Ernest, who spent time researching how to apply compressed roaring bitmap indexing to web analytics events, which forms the basis of vince's columnar storage engine. Vince is compatible with existing Plausible Analytics tracking scripts, and its official tracking script is under 1KB.
Vince is released under the AGPL-3.0 license and does not currently offer an official managed or cloud-hosted version — it is designed to be self-hosted.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Vince is aimed at developers, site owners, and privacy-conscious teams who want to run their own analytics infrastructure instead of relying on Google Analytics or a paid SaaS analytics vendor.

It suits organizations that need cookieless, GDPR/CCPA/PECR-oriented tracking without cookie consent banners, and that want to track an unlimited number of websites and events without hitting artificial plan limits.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Ships as one binary with everything included — no external dependencies — deployable via Docker, Helm charts, or a direct download.

features.title: Cookieless, privacy-friendly tracking
features.description: Designed to be GDPR, CCPA, and PECR compliant without requiring cookie consent notices.

features.title: Custom events and goal conversions
features.description: Track custom events and goal/conversion completions in addition to standard pageview metrics.

features.title: File download, outbound link, and 404 tracking
features.description: Automatically tracks file downloads, outbound link clicks, and 404 page hits.

features.title: Unlimited websites and events
features.description: No per-site or per-event caps built into the software itself, unlike many hosted analytics tiers.

features.title: Lightweight tracking script
features.description: Official tracking script is under 1KB and is compatible with Plausible Analytics' script format.

features.title: Automatic TLS
features.description: Built-in automatic TLS certificate provisioning (Let's Encrypt) for self-hosted deployments.

features.title: Columnar storage with compressed roaring bitmaps
features.description: Uses compressed roaring bitmap indexing over columnar storage for query performance at scale.

pros: Free and open source (AGPL-3.0)
Single binary makes self-hosted deployment simple compared to multi-service analytics stacks
No cookie banners needed due to privacy-first design
No artificial limits on sites or tracked events
Compatible with existing Plausible Analytics tracking scripts, easing migration
cons: No official managed/cloud-hosted plan — self-hosting is required, which demands server/ops know-how
Younger, smaller project (built by a small team/single maintainer credited) with a smaller ecosystem than Google Analytics or Plausible
No published pricing or SLA for support since it is community/self-hosted software

faqs.question: Is Vince free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vince is open source under the AGPL-3.0 license and is free to self-host.

faqs.question: Does Vince offer a hosted/cloud version?
faqs.answer: No official cloud-hosted plan is published on the vince website — it is designed for self-hosting via Docker, Helm, or a standalone binary.

faqs.question: Is Vince compatible with Plausible Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes, vince's tracking script is compatible with existing Plausible Analytics setups.

faqs.question: What license is Vince released under?
faqs.answer: Vince is released under the GNU AGPL-3.0 open-source license.

use_cases.title: Privacy-compliant analytics without cookie banners
use_cases.description: Teams needing GDPR/CCPA/PECR-friendly tracking without displaying cookie consent notices.
use_cases.audience: Web teams and companies serving EU/privacy-regulated users

use_cases.title: Self-hosted analytics with full data ownership
use_cases.description: Running analytics infrastructure on infrastructure you control instead of sending data to a third party.

use_cases.title: Migrating from Google Analytics or Plausible
use_cases.description: Replacing a paid or tiered analytics tool with an unlimited-site, single-binary self-hosted alternative.
use_cases.audience: Site owners consolidating analytics without per-tier pricing

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vince: free, open-source, self-hosted web analytics software shipped as a single binary — a privacy-friendly Google Analytics alternative.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source cataloguing and collections management software for museums and archives — not an e-signature tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Collective Access - Providence is not an e-signature tool. Providence is the back-end cataloguing and data-management component of CollectiveAccess, a free, open-source software suite for cataloguing and publishing museum and archival collections. It is a framework for the management, description, and discovery of complex digital and physical collections in museum, archival, and research contexts.

CollectiveAccess began in 2003 as a response to a lack of non-commercial, affordable, open-source solutions for digital collections management. It is maintained by the staff of Whirl-i-Gig and contributed to by the open-source community, and is distributed under the GNU GPL-3.0 license.

The suite consists of two applications: Providence, the browser-based cataloguing back end used to manage collection records, and Pawtucket2, an optional public-facing front end for publishing collections online.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Providence is used by museums, archives, libraries, historical societies, and research/catalog raisonné projects that need a configurable system for cataloguing objects, images, audio, video, documents, and 3D media.

It suits institutions of varying sizes and technical sophistication that want a free, self-hosted alternative to commercial collections-management systems, and that are willing to configure metadata standards and workflows to their needs. Whirl-i-Gig also offers paid consulting, hosting, and support services for institutions that want professional implementation help.

features.title: Configurable cataloguing
features.description: Pre-configured with popular metadata standards and supports customizable fields, views, and workflows.

features.title: Batch import/export
features.description: Batch importing and exporting of collection records, plus PDF and spreadsheet report generation.

features.title: Multi-format media support
features.description: Manages documents, images, audio, video, and 3D media formats within a single catalogue.

features.title: Multi-language localization
features.description: Supports localized user interfaces and multi-lingual metadata via locale codes for both the UI and catalogued data.

features.title: Authority/vocabulary management
features.description: Manage controlled vocabularies and authority records for people, places, subjects, and organizations.

features.title: Optional public front end (Pawtucket2)
features.description: Publish collections online with customizable facets, filters, mapping, timelines, and visualization tools, plus public commenting, tagging, and rating.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs on your own infrastructure, giving institutions full ownership and control of their collection data.

pros: Free and open source under GPL-3.0
Highly configurable to different metadata standards and institution types
Mature project in active use since 2003 with ongoing releases
Scales from small archives to large museum collections
Optional Pawtucket2 module for publishing collections publicly online
cons: Not an e-signature or document-signing tool, despite being listed in that category
Requires self-hosting and server administration to run
High configurability can create a steep learning curve for new users
Professional hosting, consulting, and support are paid add-on services, not included with the free software

faqs.question: Is Collective Access - Providence an e-signature tool?
faqs.answer: No. Providence is cataloguing and collections-management software for museums and archives, not a document e-signature product.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Providence and Pawtucket2?
faqs.answer: Providence is the back-end cataloguing application used to manage collection records; Pawtucket2 is an optional public-facing front end for publishing collections online.

faqs.question: Is CollectiveAccess free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free, open-source software released under the GPL-3.0 license.

faqs.question: Who maintains CollectiveAccess?
faqs.answer: It is maintained by the staff of Whirl-i-Gig along with contributions from the open-source community.

faqs.question: Can I get paid support or hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Whirl-i-Gig offers consulting, hosting, and support services to institutions for a fee, separate from the free open-source software.

use_cases.title: Museum collections cataloguing
use_cases.description: Cataloguing objects, artworks, and associated media with configurable metadata standards.

use_cases.title: Archive and library digital collections management
use_cases.description: Managing and describing archival and library holdings across multiple media formats.

use_cases.title: Public online collection portals
use_cases.description: Publishing searchable, browsable collections to the public using the optional Pawtucket2 front end.
use_cases.audience: Institutions wanting to share collections online

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Collective Access - Providence is free, open-source cataloguing software for museums and archives — part of the CollectiveAccess suite since 2003.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, self-hosted email archiving tool for IMAP, POP, Exchange, and JMAP mailboxes — not an e-signature product.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Eonvelope is not an e-signature or document-envelope tool. It is a free, open-source, self-hosted email preservation and archiving system that connects to IMAP, POP, Microsoft Exchange, and JMAP accounts and automatically archives mail traffic from individual folders or entire mailboxes.

The project stores messages, attachments, and correspondent information while preserving conversation threads, and provides a web interface (built as a mobile-friendly Progressive Web App) for browsing and searching the archive. It is developed by David Aderbauer and community contributors, licensed under AGPLv3, with its primary repository hosted on GitLab (created June 2025) and a read-only mirror on GitHub.

Eonvelope is deployed as a small Docker-based stack (described by the project as a compact two-container setup) and includes documentation on ReadTheDocs, active releases, and an ongoing translation effort via Weblate.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Eonvelope is aimed at self-hosting enthusiasts and privacy-conscious individuals who want to preserve and search their own email history outside of their mail provider's retention window.

It also fits small teams or home-lab users who already run other self-hosted tools, since Eonvelope integrates with services like Paperless-ngx (for documents) and Immich (for photos) directly from its web interface.

features.title: Multi-protocol mail archiving
features.description: Connects to and archives mail from IMAP, POP, Microsoft Exchange, and JMAP accounts.

features.title: Whole-account or per-folder archiving
features.description: Automatically archives mail traffic from single folders or entire mail accounts.

features.title: Searchable web interface with threading
features.description: Browse and search archived mail through a web UI that preserves conversation threads.

features.title: Mobile-friendly PWA
features.description: The web interface is built as a Progressive Web App for mobile-friendly access.

features.title: Integrations with self-hosted apps
features.description: Can send attachments to Paperless-ngx or images to Immich directly from the archive.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: Supports MFA for securing access to the archive.

features.title: Compact Docker deployment
features.description: Deploys as a small two-container Docker stack, with setup the project describes as taking under five minutes.

pros: Free and open source (AGPLv3)
Simple, quick self-hosted setup with a small Docker footprint
Integrates with other self-hosted tools such as Paperless-ngx and Immich
Actively developed with public documentation and regular releases
cons: Not an e-signature or document-signing tool, despite being listed in that category
Very young project (created 2025) with a small maintainer/contributor base and limited track record
Requires self-hosting and Docker/server administration knowledge
No hosted/managed version — self-hosting is the only deployment option

faqs.question: Is Eonvelope an e-signature tool?
faqs.answer: No. Eonvelope is a self-hosted email archiving and preservation system, not a document e-signature product.

faqs.question: What email protocols does Eonvelope support?
faqs.answer: IMAP, POP, Microsoft Exchange, and JMAP.

faqs.question: Is Eonvelope free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is released under the AGPLv3 open-source license and is self-hosted.

faqs.question: How is Eonvelope deployed?
faqs.answer: Via a small Docker-based stack the project describes as a two-container setup.

faqs.question: Who maintains Eonvelope?
faqs.answer: Developer David Aderbauer and the Eonvelope Contributors, with an active GitLab repository (mirrored read-only on GitHub) created in mid-2025.

use_cases.title: Personal email preservation
use_cases.description: Archiving personal mailboxes for long-term, searchable access outside a provider's retention limits.

use_cases.title: Searchable long-term mail records
use_cases.description: Keeping a searchable, threaded archive of mail for small teams or organizations.
use_cases.audience: Small teams needing independent mail record-keeping

use_cases.title: Home-lab / self-hosted stack integration
use_cases.description: Pairing an email archive with other self-hosted services like Paperless-ngx and Immich.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Eonvelope is a free, open-source, self-hosted email archive for IMAP, POP, Exchange, and JMAP mailboxes — not an e-signature tool.

enrichment.short_description: Omeka S is a free, open-source platform for publishing and linking digital cultural heritage collections across multiple sites.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Omeka S is a next-generation, open-source web publishing platform for institutions that want to connect digital cultural heritage collections with other resources online. It is maintained by Digital Scholar (the Corporation for Digital Scholarship), which grew out of the Roy Rosenzweig Center for History and New Media (RRCHNM) at George Mason University, the original home of the Omeka project.

Unlike its predecessor Omeka Classic, Omeka S is built around linked open data: content is modeled using RDF and standard vocabularies, and a single installation can manage and publish multiple connected sites. It is distributed under the GNU GPL and is self-hosted, requiring a server running PHP and MySQL.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Omeka S is built for cultural heritage and academic institutions — libraries, archives, museums, galleries, and university digital-humanities projects — that need to describe, organize, and publish digital collections using structured, linkable metadata rather than a general-purpose CMS.

features.title: Multi-site management
features.description: Run and manage multiple public-facing sites from a single Omeka S installation and admin backend.

features.title: Linked open data
features.description: Resources are described with RDF and standard vocabularies, making collections publishable as linked open data.

features.title: Items, item sets, and media
features.description: Core content is modeled as items, item sets, and media, described using customizable resource templates.

features.title: DPLA-ready templates
features.description: Resource templates designed to align with Digital Public Library of America (DPLA) metadata requirements.

features.title: Module ecosystem
features.description: Extend functionality with modules such as CSV Import, Mapping, IIIF Presentation, Data Visualization, Scripto, and Zotero integration.

features.title: Responsive themes
features.description: Customize the public-facing presentation of each site with responsive, configurable themes.

features.title: Role-based site permissions
features.description: Assign user roles and permissions that vary by site within the same installation.

pros: Completely free and open source under the GNU GPL
Strong support for linked open data and semantic web standards
Flexible metadata modeling via resource templates and vocabularies
Extensible through a large ecosystem of community modules
Actively developed on GitHub with an established institutional backer (Digital Scholar)
cons: Requires self-hosting a PHP/MySQL server and ongoing technical maintenance
No official managed hosting or paid support tier from the core team listed on the site
Steeper learning curve for non-technical users compared to general-purpose website builders

faqs.question: Is Omeka S free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Omeka S is open-source software released under the GNU GPL license and can be downloaded and self-hosted at no cost.

faqs.question: Is Omeka S an e-signature tool?
faqs.answer: No. Omeka S is a web publishing platform for digital cultural heritage collections, not an e-signature or document-signing product.

faqs.question: Who maintains Omeka S?
faqs.answer: Omeka S is maintained by Digital Scholar (the Corporation for Digital Scholarship), which originated as part of the Roy Rosenzweig Center for History and New Media at George Mason University.

faqs.question: What are Omeka S modules?
faqs.answer: Modules are optional plugins that extend core functionality, such as CSV Import, Mapping, IIIF Presentation, and Zotero integration.

faqs.question: Can one installation run multiple sites?
faqs.answer: Yes, Omeka S is designed so a single installation can host and manage multiple connected public sites.

use_cases.title: Digital library and archive publishing
use_cases.description: Libraries and archives use Omeka S to describe and publish digitized collections with structured, linkable metadata.

use_cases.title: Museum digital collections
use_cases.description: Museums publish object collections online using linked open data and DPLA-ready templates.

use_cases.title: Digital humanities projects
use_cases.description: Universities and researchers build multi-site digital humanities projects on a single shared installation.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Omeka S is a free, open-source, self-hosted platform for publishing and linking digital cultural heritage collections online.

enrichment.short_description: Discount Bandit is a self-hosted price tracker that monitors Amazon, AliExpress, eBay and more, alerting you to price drops.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Discount Bandit is a free, self-hosted, multi-user price-tracking application built with Laravel and Filament PHP. It monitors product prices across retailers including Amazon, AliExpress, eBay, Walmart, Newegg, and Noon, as well as custom stores, and tracks price history and stock availability over time.

The project is developed in the open on GitHub (cybrarist/discount-bandit), where it has an active release history (version 4.0.4 as of mid-2026) and a Discord community. It can be deployed directly with PHP, Composer, and MySQL/SQLite, or via Docker, Docker Compose, or Portainer using published images on Docker Hub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Discount Bandit is aimed at self-hosting enthusiasts and deal hunters who want to track prices across multiple online stores and receive custom alerts without relying on a third-party commercial price-tracking service.

features.title: Multi-store price tracking
features.description: Track prices on Amazon, AliExpress, eBay, Walmart, Newegg, Noon, and custom stores from one dashboard.

features.title: Price history and change monitoring
features.description: Records historical price data so you can see how a product's price has changed over time.

features.title: Stock availability alerts
features.description: Get notified when a tracked product's stock status changes, not just when the price drops.

features.title: Customizable alert criteria
features.description: Configure multiple notification rules based on your own price and availability criteria.

features.title: Multi-user support
features.description: The web dashboard supports multiple users tracking their own products and alerts.

features.title: Flexible self-hosted deployment
features.description: Install directly with PHP/Composer/MySQL or SQLite, or deploy via Docker, Docker Compose, or Portainer.

features.title: Companion browser extension (planned)
features.description: A browser extension for adding tracked products directly from store pages is listed as coming soon.

pros: Free to run and fully self-hosted, keeping your tracked data private
Supports several major retailers plus custom stores
Actively developed with regular GitHub releases
Multiple deployment options including Docker and Docker Compose
Multi-user support and a Discord community for help
cons: Requires self-hosting knowledge (PHP, Composer, a database, and a web server)
No license file is published in the GitHub repository, so reuse/redistribution terms are unclear
The browser companion extension is not yet released
Smaller community compared to established commercial price trackers

faqs.question: Is Discount Bandit free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is a free, self-hosted application with no published pricing plans.

faqs.question: Which stores does it support?
faqs.answer: Amazon, AliExpress, eBay, Walmart, Newegg, and Noon are supported out of the box, along with custom store configurations.

faqs.question: How do I install it?
faqs.answer: You can install it directly with PHP, Composer, and MySQL or SQLite, or deploy it using Docker, Docker Compose, or Portainer.

faqs.question: Is there a browser extension?
faqs.answer: A companion browser extension for adding products directly from store pages is planned but listed as coming soon.

faqs.question: Is Discount Bandit open source?
faqs.answer: The source code is publicly available on GitHub, though the repository does not currently publish an explicit open-source license.

use_cases.title: Personal deal hunting
use_cases.description: Track wishlist items across multiple stores and get alerted when the price hits your target.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious price tracking
use_cases.description: Self-host your own tracker instead of relying on a third-party commercial service with your purchase interests.

use_cases.title: Shared household or team tracking
use_cases.description: Multiple users can track and manage their own product alerts on one shared instance.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Discount Bandit is a free, self-hosted price tracker for Amazon, AliExpress, eBay and more with custom alerts and Docker deploy.

enrichment.short_description: flowctl is a free, open-source, self-hosted workflow automation platform with YAML flows, approval gates, and a single binary.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: flowctl is a self-service workflow execution platform distributed as a single binary (github.com/cvhariharan/flowctl), licensed under Apache 2.0. It lets teams define workflows in a built-in editor or as YAML/HUML configuration, and run them with real-time streaming logs, scheduled cron execution, and approval gates for sensitive operations.

PostgreSQL is the only external dependency; everything else ships in the binary, and deployment is available via Docker or as a standalone executable. The project is actively maintained, with 44 releases and commits as recent as mid-2026, and it offers a public live demo at demo.flowctl.net alongside documentation at flowctl.net/docs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: flowctl targets engineering and operations teams that need controlled, auditable self-service automation — for example running SSH-based provisioning or infrastructure tasks — with team-based organization, role-based access control, and approval workflows for sensitive actions.

features.title: Single-binary deployment
features.description: Ships as one binary with PostgreSQL as the only external dependency, simplifying self-hosted setup.

features.title: YAML/HUML workflow definitions
features.description: Define flows through a built-in editor or as YAML/HUML configuration files with inputs and approvals.

features.title: Approval gates
features.description: Require explicit approval before sensitive workflow steps or operations can execute.

features.title: Team-based RBAC
features.description: Organize users into teams with role-based access control across user, operator, reviewer, and admin roles.

features.title: OIDC-based SSO
features.description: Authenticate users through OIDC single sign-on integration.

features.title: Remote execution
features.description: Run workflow steps remotely, including SSH-based execution across multiple nodes.

features.title: Encrypted secret management
features.description: Store credentials and secrets encrypted at rest for use within workflows.

features.title: Scheduled and real-time execution
features.description: Trigger flows on a cron schedule and monitor them with real-time streaming logs and notifications via email or webhooks.

pros: Free and open source under the permissive Apache 2.0 license
Lightweight self-hosted deployment with only PostgreSQL as a dependency
Built-in approval gates and RBAC suited for sensitive operational tasks
Public live demo lets you evaluate it before installing
Actively developed with frequent releases and recent commit activity
cons: Young project (created in late 2025), so the ecosystem and community are still small
Requires self-hosting and a PostgreSQL database, with associated operational overhead
Documented integrations are currently limited to SSH, email, and webhooks

faqs.question: Is flowctl free?
faqs.answer: Yes, flowctl is free and open source under the Apache 2.0 license, and is self-hosted.

faqs.question: What does flowctl require to run?
faqs.answer: PostgreSQL is the only external dependency; everything else is included in the single binary.

faqs.question: Can I try flowctl before installing it?
faqs.answer: Yes, a live demo is available at demo.flowctl.net.

faqs.question: Does flowctl support scheduled automation?
faqs.answer: Yes, flows can be triggered on a cron schedule in addition to manual or approval-gated execution.

faqs.question: How does flowctl handle access control?
faqs.answer: It uses team-based organization with role-based access control (user, operator, reviewer, admin) and supports OIDC-based SSO.

use_cases.title: Infrastructure and SSH automation
use_cases.description: Run SSH-based provisioning or infrastructure tasks with approval gates before execution.

use_cases.title: Self-service internal tooling
use_cases.description: Expose controlled, auditable workflows that other teams can trigger themselves without direct system access.

use_cases.title: Scheduled operational tasks
use_cases.description: Automate recurring processes using cron-based scheduling and real-time execution logs.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: flowctl is a free, open-source workflow automation platform: YAML-defined flows, approval gates, RBAC, single-binary deploy.
